top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of the ClawGym-SynData pipeline, which generates tasks from persona-driven and skill-grounded sources, prepares task resources, designs hybrid verification, filters samples through quality assessment, and constructs training and benchmark data.

top_papers.title: Lyapunov-Guided Self-Alignment: Test-Time Adaptation for Offline Safe Reinforcement Learning
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces SAS, a transformer-based framework for test-time adaptation in offline safe reinforcement learning that avoids retraining. The core method involves the agent generating imagined trajectories and selecting those satisfying a Lyapunov condition, using these safe segments as in-context prompts to guide behavior alignment. This approach effectively translates Lyapunov constraints into control-invariant prompts, significantly reducing failure rates while preserving performance on safety benchmarks.
top_papers.figure_caption: SAS overview . From a fixed initial state, the transformer imagines multiple rollouts, flags risky state–action pairs using the Lyapunov condition with ( ✖ ), and extracts a safe segment as a prompt to guide the real test-time trajectory (hazards: black ○ \( \bigcirc \) , blue ◇ \( \Diamond \) ; goal: green ⚫ ).

top_papers.title: Preserving Disagreement: Architectural Heterogeneity and Coherence Validation in Multi-Agent Policy Simulation
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces the **AI Council**, a three-phase deliberation framework designed to combat artificial consensus in LLM-based multi-agent policy simulation. The core contribution is demonstrating that **architectural heterogeneity** (using different small LLMs for each value perspective) significantly reduces convergence toward a single policy choice compared to homogeneous setups. This highlights the importance of model diversity when simulating subjective, value-based disagreements.

top_papers.title: TDD Governance for Multi-Agent Code Generation via Prompt Engineering
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces an AI-native TDD governance framework that operationalizes classical TDD principles as structured constraints within a multi-agent code generation workflow. It formalizes these principles into a machine-readable manifesto enforced across planning, generation, and validation stages using prompt engineering. The core contribution is establishing deterministic workflow governance—including phase ordering and bounded repair loops—to enhance the stability and reproducibility of LLM-generated code.
top_papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Illustrative manifesto entries and their governance structure.

top_papers.title: Unified 4D World Action Modeling from Video Priors with Asynchronous Denoising
top_papers.abstract: X-WAM is a Unified 4D World Model that integrates real-time robotic action execution with high-fidelity 4D world synthesis (video and 3D reconstruction). It leverages pretrained video diffusion models to predict future multi-view RGB-D videos, efficiently incorporating spatial information via a lightweight adaptation of the diffusion model's structure. The method further employs Asynchronous Noise Sampling (ANS) to optimize the trade-off between generation quality and action decoding efficiency.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of X-WAM. Top: X-WAM is a unified 4D World Action Model that jointly predicts future multi-view RGB-D videos and robot actions from video priors, featuring a lightweight depth adaptation module for spatial reconstruction and Asynchronous Noise Sampling (ANS) for efficient action decoding. Bottom: X-WAM surpasses existing methods in policy success rate on RoboCasa and RoboTwin 2.0, produces high-fidelity 4D reconstruction and generation, and enables real-time execution deployment on physical robots.

top_papers.title: HealthNLP_Retrievers at ArchEHR-QA 2026: Cascaded LLM Pipeline for Grounded Clinical Question Answering
top_papers.abstract: The HealthNLP\_Retrievers team developed a cascaded LLM pipeline using Gemini 2.5 Pro for grounded clinical question answering over EHRs. Their core method involves four stages: query reformulation, heuristic-based evidence scoring for recall, and a grounded response generator that synthesizes answers strictly from retrieved evidence. This approach aims to accurately interpret patient questions and generate understandable, evidence-based answers from complex clinical notes.
top_papers.figure_caption: Workflow of the HealthNLP_Retrievers multi-stage cascaded pipeline.

top_papers.title: MoRFI: Monotonic Sparse Autoencoder Feature Identification
top_papers.abstract: The paper introduces **MoRFI (Monotonic Sparse Autoencoder Feature Identification)** to analyze how fine-tuning exacerbates hallucinations in LLMs by introducing new knowledge. The core method involves controlled fine-tuning experiments on various LLMs with new QA datasets, observing that incremental knowledge introduction and prolonged training increase hallucinations. MoRFI then leverages pre-trained Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) to identify specific, causally contributing latent directions linked to these factual errors.

top_papers.title: PAINT: Partial-Solution Adaptive Interpolated Training for Self-Distilled Reasoners
top_papers.abstract: PAINT introduces Partial-solution Adaptive Interpolated Training for self-distilled LLM reasoners. The core method adaptively masks the verified solution based on its overlap with the student's current rollout, then applies a small energy-space interpolation to generate dense, context-aware training targets. This approach aims to balance the informativeness of supervised targets with the on-policy relevance of self-distillation.
top_papers.figure_caption: PAINT training pipeline. PAINT samples an on-policy rollout, uses rollout-reference overlap \( \alpha \) to form a suffix-masked solution y ~ ⋆ \( \tilde{y}^{\star} \) , re-scores the same prefixes with a fixed privileged view, and applies small energy interpolation only on entropy-mismatch positions.

top_papers.title: OCR-Memory: Optical Context Retrieval for Long-Horizon Agent Memory
top_papers.abstract: OCR-Memory addresses the token-budget limitations of text-based agent memory by leveraging the visual modality as a high-density experience representation. The core method involves rendering historical agent trajectories into annotated images, which are then retrieved using a "locate-and-transcribe" paradigm based on visual anchors. This approach allows agents to retain arbitrarily long histories with minimal prompt overhead during retrieval, significantly improving long-horizon performance.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of the OCR-Memory. The system enables long-horizon agent memory by storing interaction histories as compressed multi-resolution images (left). To retrieve information, we employ a Locate-and-Transcribe paradigm: the model scans the visual history annotated with Set-of-Mark (SoM) visual anchors (center) to predict the index of relevant segments. Finally, the verbatim text corresponding to the selected index is deterministically fetched (right), avoiding generation-based hallucinations and minimizing token usage.

top_papers.title: SAGE: A Strategy-Aware Graph-Enhanced Generation Framework For Online Counseling
top_papers.abstract: SAGE is a novel framework that enhances LLMs for online counseling by integrating structured clinical knowledge. It constructs a heterogeneous graph unifying conversational dynamics with psychological theories to inform intervention. This structure guides a Next Strategy Classifier and a Graph-Aware Attention mechanism, which conditions the LLM to generate responses with appropriate clinical depth and strategic awareness.
top_papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Fictitious session snippet with psychological categories and intervention strategies presented.

top_papers.title: Building Persona-Based Agents On Demand: Tailoring Multi-Agent Workflows to User Needs
top_papers.abstract: This paper proposes a method for dynamically generating persona-based agents at runtime to tailor multi-agent workflows to specific user needs and task contexts. The core contribution is a pipeline that systematically crafts AI personas in real-time, moving beyond fixed agent architectures to enable more efficient and contextually appropriate automation.
top_papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. A visual representation of the pipeline’s steps

top_papers.title: Can AI Be a Good Peer Reviewer? A Survey of Peer Review Process, Evaluation, and the Future
top_papers.abstract: This survey comprehensively reviews existing and emerging techniques for leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) across the entire academic peer review pipeline, from initial review generation to rebuttal drafting and final decision support. The core contribution is a systematic synthesis of methodologies, evaluation metrics, datasets, and ethical considerations, offering practical guidance for integrating AI into scholarly communication workflows.

top_papers.title: Exploring Interaction Paradigms for LLM Agents in Scientific Visualization
top_papers.abstract: This paper explores and compares three interaction paradigms—domain-specific, computer-use, and general-purpose coding agents—for LLM agents tasked with generating scientific visualization workflows from natural language. The study evaluates agent performance based on visualization quality, efficiency, and cost across various interaction modalities like code scripts and API calls. The core contribution is revealing clear tradeoffs between these paradigms, showing that general-purpose coding agents offer the highest success rates despite higher computational costs.
top_papers.figure_caption: The 15 representative ParaView visualization tasks from SciVisAgentBench.

top_papers.title: From Unstructured Recall to Schema-Grounded Memory: Reliable AI Memory via Iterative, Schema-Aware Extraction
top_papers.abstract: This paper proposes a **Schema-Grounded Memory** system to move AI memory beyond simple text retrieval toward a reliable system of record. The core method involves an **iterative, schema-aware write path** that decomposes memory ingestion into structured object and field extraction with validation. This shifts the burden of reliability to the write process, enabling constrained queries and supporting complex operations like updates, deletions, and negative queries.
top_papers.figure_caption: Iterative extraction pipeline: staged decisions with validation gates and local retries.

top_papers.title: Intern-Atlas: A Methodological Evolution Graph as Research Infrastructure for AI Scientists
top_papers.abstract: Intern-Atlas introduces a methodological evolution graph to address the document-centric limitations of current research infrastructure by explicitly mapping how AI research methods emerge and adapt. The core method automatically identifies method entities and infers lineage relationships, capturing the transitions and bottlenecks driving methodological innovation. This structured graph serves as crucial research infrastructure, enabling reliable reconstruction of method evolution topologies, especially for AI research agents.
top_papers.figure_caption: From paper lists to an agent-readable methodology atlas. Citation-based discovery tools (left) leave method evolution ( Transformer → \( \to \) BERT / GPT / ViT ) implicit in human expertise. Intern-Atlas (middle) makes it explicit as a typed graph with causal edge labels and verbatim bottleneck-to-mechanism evidence, providing an LLM agent (right) with direct queries for method lineage, bottleneck evidence, idea evaluation, and idea generation.

top_papers.title: KellyBench: A Benchmark for Long-Horizon Sequential Decision Making
top_papers.abstract: KellyBench is introduced as a novel benchmark environment simulating the long-horizon, non-stationary challenge of sports betting on the English Premier League season. The core method involves tasking agents with maximizing long-term bankroll growth using historical data, requiring them to build adaptive ML models to find market edges. The contribution highlights that even frontier models struggle significantly, losing money on average, underscoring the difficulty of real-world sequential decision-making beyond narrow procedural tasks.
top_papers.figure_caption: Model Performance on KellyBench . KellyBench tasks models with developing machine learning betting strategies for the 2023/24 English Premier League season with the goal of maximising long-term bankroll growth. No model makes a return on average across 5 seeds. Models also fail to adapt strategies in response to failure. Initial bankroll is normalised to £100K for display purposes.

top_papers.title: LLMs as ASP Programmers: Self-Correction Enables Task-Agnostic Nonmonotonic Reasoning
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces "LLM+ASP," a novel framework that translates natural language into Answer Set Programming (ASP) to enable task-agnostic, nonmonotonic reasoning for LLMs. The core method involves an automated self-correction loop that uses structured feedback to refine the ASP translation, overcoming limitations of existing neuro-symbolic approaches that often rely on monotonic logics. This contributes a generalizable system capable of handling complex, defeasible reasoning without requiring task-specific engineering.
top_papers.figure_caption: LLM+ASP Pipeline

top_papers.title: Modeling Clinical Concern Trajectories in Language Model Agents
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces a lightweight architecture for LLM agents that integrates a memoryless risk encoder over time using first- and second-order dynamics to generate a continuous "escalation pressure signal." This method moves beyond abrupt, threshold-driven behavior by producing smooth, anticipatory concern trajectories that reveal accumulating risk *before* a critical escalation point. The core contribution is enabling human-in-the-loop monitoring by providing visibility into gradual clinical unease, without granting the agent independent clinical authority.
